title: rosa coriifolia subvar. campicola
intro: Rosa coriifolia subvar. campicola, also known as the 'Mangy Rose', is a subvariety of the Rosa coriifolia plant, a species of flowering plant in the rose family. The Rosa coriifolia subvar. campicola has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant are said to include the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Rosa coriifolia subvar. campicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
